VaccineValidateR 0.0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles

Filter and Process Immunization Data

Source: R/select_immunizations.R
select_immunizations.Rd

Filters and processes immunization data by mapping antigens to CVX codes, ensuring that the correct immunization data is processed and returned.

Usage

select_immunizations(
  immunization_data,
  lim_cvx_map = NULL,
  antigen_to_eval = c("ALL"),
  verbose = TRUE,
  date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",
  study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",
  immunization_code_column_name = "CVX",
  local_immunization_identifier_column_name = "LIM",
  immunization_product_name_column_name = "PRODUCT",
  immunization_date_given_column_name = "DATE_GIVEN",
  age_at_immunization_column_name = "AGE_IMM_GIVEN"
)

Arguments

immunization_data

A data.table of immunizations containing at minimum STUDY_ID, PRODUCT, and either DATE_GIVEN or AGE_IMM_GIVEN.

lim_cvx_map

Optional data.table for local-code→CVX mappings. Default is NULL. Used if 'CVX' is not present in the immunization data.

antigen_to_eval

Character vector of antigens to evaluate (e.g., c('POLIO','MMR')) or 'ALL' (case-insensitive).

verbose

Logical indicating whether to print log messages. Default is TRUE.

date_of_birth_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing date of birth. Default is 'DOB'.

study_id_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing study ID. Default is 'STUDY_ID'.

immunization_code_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing CVX codes. Default is 'CVX'.

local_immunization_identifier_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing local immunization identifiers. Default is 'LIM'.

immunization_product_name_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing immunization product names. Default is 'PRODUCT'.

immunization_date_given_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing the date the immunization was given. Default is 'DATE_GIVEN'.

age_at_immunization_column_name

Character string specifying the column containing age at immunization. Default is 'AGE_IMM_GIVEN'.

Value

A data.table containing the refined and filtered immunization data with an added attribute processed indicating the table is ready. An attribute antigens_used (character vector) is attached, recording which antigens were evaluated.

Details

Antigen selection has special handling for live, non-enteral vaccines (e.g., MMR, VZV, intranasal INFLUENZA). If any of these are included in antigen_to_eval or present in the dataset, all such live antigens are retained together. This ensures that live-virus scheduling rules (e.g., spacing between doses) are preserved during downstream validate_immunizations.

Examples

library(data.table)

# Example immunization data
imm_dt <- data.table(
  STUDY_ID   = c(1, 2, 3, 3),
  DOB        = as.Date(c("2020-01-15", "2019-07-30", "2018-03-15", "2018-03-15")),
  PRODUCT    = c("MMR Vax", "DTaP", "Polio", "Varicella"),
  CVX        = c(03, 20, 10, 21),           # simplified CVX codes
  DATE_GIVEN = as.Date(c("2021-02-01", "2020-09-01", "2020-05-01", "2021-06-01"))
)

# Select all immunizations
selected_all <- select_immunizations(imm_dt)
#> CVX codes employed to determine antigens within immunizations. Merging data. Please Wait...
#> Content restricted to antigens of interest. Finalizing content.
#> This table can be sent to validate_immunizations function directly and will not be reprocessed.
#> Do not change 'processed' attribute. N=4
print(selected_all)
#>    STUDY_ID        DOB   PRODUCT   CVX DATE_GIVEN  POLIO    HIB    PCV   HEPA
#>       <num>     <Date>    <char> <num>     <Date> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl>
#> 1:        1 2020-01-15   MMR Vax     3 2021-02-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 2:        2 2019-07-30      DTaP    20 2020-09-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 3:        3 2018-03-15     Polio    10 2020-05-01   TRUE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 4:        3 2018-03-15 Varicella    21 2021-06-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#>      HEPB    HPV    MCV   MENB TETANUS   ROTA  COVID    RSV    MMR    VZV
#>    <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl>  <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl>
#> 1:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE  FALSE
#> 2:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE    TRUE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 3:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 4:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE
#>    INFLUENZA GIVEN_STATUS ADMIN_LOCATION
#>       <lgcl>        <num>         <lgcl>
#> 1:     FALSE            1             NA
#> 2:     FALSE            1             NA
#> 3:     FALSE            1             NA
#> 4:     FALSE            1             NA

# Select only POLIO and MMR antigens
selected_subset <- select_immunizations(imm_dt, antigen_to_eval = c("POLIO", "MMR"))#will also return Varicella(VZV) since any live nonenteral antigen forces all to be kept
#> CVX codes employed to determine antigens within immunizations. Merging data. Please Wait...
#> Content restricted to antigens of interest. Finalizing content.
#> This table can be sent to validate_immunizations function directly and will not be reprocessed.
#> Do not change 'processed' attribute. N=3
print(selected_subset)
#>    STUDY_ID        DOB   PRODUCT   CVX DATE_GIVEN  POLIO    MMR    VZV
#>       <num>     <Date>    <char> <num>     <Date> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl>
#> 1:        1 2020-01-15   MMR Vax     3 2021-02-01  FALSE   TRUE  FALSE
#> 2:        3 2018-03-15     Polio    10 2020-05-01   TRUE  FALSE  FALSE
#> 3:        3 2018-03-15 Varicella    21 2021-06-01  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE
#>    INFLUENZA GIVEN_STATUS ADMIN_LOCATION
#>       <lgcl>        <num>         <lgcl>
#> 1:     FALSE            1             NA
#> 2:     FALSE            1             NA
#> 3:     FALSE            1             NA
